items.title: An upper ceiling prediction of Neemias Queata’s career barring injuries (assuming 70 games per season): Year 1: 3.4ppg, Year 2: 4.2ppg, Year 3-8: 17ppg, Year 9-15: 31ppg, Year 16-20: 40ppg. That's 42,512 points over those 20 years. To put this into perspective, Lebron had 38652 points by year 20.
items.author: Mysterious_Piglet562
items.body_excerpt: A lot of this is clearly hoping a lot of things go right, and often times, for anyone to muster up a 20 year NBA career, something did go right. It also assumes he reaches most of his potential. But I think assuming he stays healthy and has a long career, these numbers are not…

items.title: The Sky are a bit better than their record, but it is really tough watching these stretches where they completely deflate due to veterans making mistakes and disengaging from plays.
items.author: Julia Poe
items.body_excerpt:
The Sky are a bit better than their record, but it is really tough watching these stretches where they completely deflate due to veterans making mistakes and disengaging from plays. 

It’s different if the young kids are making/learning from mistakes but there’s no real timeline for growth here.

Factoring in all the agreed-to trades, plus the trades that were executed around draft picks/rights at the 2026 NBA Draft, we're already at 20 total trades for the 2026 offseason!

Sign of the times that extensions and trades are the way business gets done in the NBA nowadays.

items.title: [Plaschke] “Kawhi was the worst free-agent signing in LA sports history. He has abused an organization, alienated a fan base and basically collected nearly $300 m of Ballmer’s money and burned it. He has been injured, apathetic,entitled, disconnected and generally nothing but a pain in the backside”
items.author: shreeharis
items.body_excerpt: Source: https://www.latimes.com/sports/lakers/story/2026-06-28/clippers-kawhi-leonard-was-worst-free-agent-signing-la-history Seven years later, I’m asking the same question, only it sounds vastly different. Kawhi Leonard? Are you kidding me? Seven years later, Leonard has…
